facade_colour: warm red-brown brick with dark brown trim

hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Semi-detached, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties have retained many of their original building features including first-storey bay windows, brick cladding, and decorative brickwork. 80 Bellevue Avenue also retains a projecting bay and cross-gabled roofline. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red-brown brick with dark brown painted trim
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Good condition overall. Brick appears well-maintained. Decorative brickwork and bay window intact. Dark brown/near-black trim on bargeboard and window frames.
photo_observations.window_details: Second floor has two tall windows in projecting bay. Ground floor has two windows in bay. Gable has single window. White/light-coloured sash windows. Segmental arched brick heads on second-floor bay windows.
photo_observations.door_details: Light-coloured/cream front door visible beside bay window, with small transom above
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative brick cornice at eave line with bracketed detail
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Canted three-sided bay spanning ground and second floor, brick construction with segmental arched window heads. This is the defining feature per HCD statement.
photo_observations.porch_details: Low stoop with 2-3 steps, no covered porch
photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative brick courses between floors, segmental brick arches over bay windows, ornamental bargeboard at gable peak. Buff/cream brick accent bands visible in decorative courses.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Cross-gable with steep pitch, dark shingles
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Minimal alterations visible. Building retains strong heritage character. Windows may be replacements but maintain original proportions.
photo_observations.notes: Well-preserved 2.5-storey Victorian bay-and-gable semi-detached house. Warm red-brown brick with decorative brickwork including string courses and segmental arches. Two-storey projecting bay with cross-gable roof above. Dark brown trim. Consistent with HCD description as retaining projecting bay and cross-gabled roofline.

notes: Well-preserved 2.5-storey Victorian bay-and-gable semi-detached. Warm red-brown brick, dark brown trim. Two-storey projecting bay with cross-gable. Decorative brickwork string courses and segmental arches.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include bay window, original windows, decorative brick.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties have retained many of their original building features including first-storey bay windows, brick cladding, and decorative brickwork. 80 Bellevue Avenue also retains a projecting bay and cross-gabled roofline.

facade_description: Semi-detached three-storey bay-and-gable in warm red-brown brick with dark brown trim under a cross-gable roof. Features include a double-height three-sided projecting bay and decorative bargeboards. Minimal alterations visible. Building retains strong heritage character. Windows may be replacements but maintain original proportions. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties have retained many of their original building features including first-storey bay windows, brick cladding, and decorative brickwork. 80 Bellevue Avenue also retains a projecting bay and cross-gabled roofline.
